title: quercus lusitanica subsp. mirbeckii
intro: Quercus lusitanica subsp. mirbeckii, also known as Portuguese oak, is a subspecies of evergreen oak tree native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, inflammation, and as an antiseptic. The leaves of the Quercus lusitanica subsp. mirbeckii contain tannins and flavonoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, providing health benefits such as reducing oxidative stress and improving cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Quercus lusitanica subsp. mirbeckii, including infusions and tinctures, have been used in traditional medicine to promote digestive health and support the immune system.
